name: appify-x-listening
description: Placeholder skill for future Appify API integration. Will enable real-time social listening on Twitter/X for trendjacking and conversation monitoring. Currently not active — use social-listening skill with WebSearch as an alternative.

name: b2b-proposal
description: Component toolkit for generating adaptive B2B proposals. Not a fixed template — provides building blocks that Poppy selects from based on meeting context, client tone, and relationship stage. Use when asked to "write a proposal", "generate proposal", "create B2B proposal", or "proposal for [client]".

name: brief-writing
description: Create comprehensive static ad creative briefs with visual direction for designers. Use this skill as Phase 3 of Maya's workflow to transform concepts into designer-ready briefs. Specializes in testimonial formats, problem-central formats, and comparison formats for 1200x1200px static Meta ads.

name: competitor-context
description: This skill should be used when Maya needs competitive positioning context for writing ads that differentiate Superpower from testing-only companies. Use when asked to "compare against competitors", "write comparison ads", "differentiate from Function", or needs context on why Superpower delivers outcomes while competitors only deliver data. CRITICAL - Never name competitors in actual ad copy.

name: compliance-check
description: This skill should be used when the user asks to "check compliance", "review for FDA/FTC", "compliance review", "check health claims", "is this copy compliant", or needs to review ad copy for regulatory compliance in health-tech marketing. Provides RED/YELLOW/GREEN flagging and aggressive-but-defensible rewrites.

name: concept-matrix
description: This skill should be used when the user asks to "create a concept matrix", "generate ad concepts", "build creative variations", "systematic concept development", "Andromeda-ready concepts", or needs to produce high-volume creative concepts from insights. Provides methodology for generating 20-50+ concepts per campaign.

name: data-insights
description: Create original data-driven social content using Superpower's member data via Zuko (FHIR data bot). Covers querying, chart design spec, caption frameworks, and compliance. Use when asked to "pull data for social", "create a data post", "Superpower Data series", or when Sandy/Tom/Iris/Noah need original member data insights for content.

name: email-copy-generation
description: Generate production-ready email copy for Superpower using email-specific frameworks (AIDA, PAS, BAB, Quest, APP, SAP). Outputs 5-10 subject line variations, body copy, CTA, and compliance-ready footer. Use when asked to "write email", "email copy", "email campaign" after email brief is defined.

name: email-sequence-builder
description: Design multi-touch email sequences (welcome, nurture, win-back) for Superpower. Outputs sequence strategy, timing recommendations, and email briefs for each touch. Use when asked to "create email sequence", "welcome series", "nurture campaign", "email automation".

name: figma-canvas
description: Build Figma-ready fixed-canvas designs (charts, paid ads, social cards) using the Superpower production design system. Provides component patterns, chart templates, and ad layouts that match superpower.com pixel-perfectly. Use when Felix or any agent needs to build a visual for Figma capture.

name: funnel-builder
description: Build interactive funnel pages (quizzes, calculators, multi-step flows) using the Superpower design system. Dark theme, 4-screen flow, interactive logic, psychology badges. Use when asked to "build a funnel", "create a quiz", "make a calculator", "design an interactive tool", or any conversion funnel mockup.

name: g
description: This skill should be used when the user types "g" or "/g". Outputs the Claude Code command to launch with the growth-ai-agents plugin.

name: ghost
description: Launch the Superpower Ghost overlay — a translucent floating screen coach powered by Claude. Use when the user says "launch ghost", "start ghost", "open ghost", "run ghost", or "/ghost".

name: google-ads-audit
description: Pull Google Ads data, run weekly/monthly audits, create campaigns and asset groups, pause underperformers, and generate performance reports. Uses the Google Ads API via Python scripts.

name: insight-generation
description: This skill should be used when the user asks to "generate insights", "research pain points", "find audience insights", "analyze Reddit for insights", "discover customer truths", or needs to extract actionable insights from qualitative research for ad creative. Provides methodology for turning Reddit research into strategic insights mapped to desires.

name: instagram-core-formats
description: Use when creating Instagram content for Superpower. Defines layout, writing style, and sourcing guidelines for each of the 5 core formats. Reference during both ideation and production phases.

name: instagram-weekly-briefing
description: This skill should be used when the user asks to "create Instagram briefs", "write weekly content", "develop post briefs", "generate Instagram copy", or needs to turn selected topics into full content briefs with on-asset copy and captions. Phase 2 of Iris's workflow. Prerequisite: user has selected topics from the research phase.

name: krill
description: This skill should be used when the user says "/krill", "competitive analysis", "SEM competitive report", "ad intelligence report", or asks to analyze a health/rx/telehealth keyword's competitive landscape. Pulls SpyFu ad history, DataForSEO search volume, and optionally Google Ads data to produce a NYT-style intelligence article with Plotly.js visualizations, published to Vercel.

name: landing-page-builder
description: Build production-grade landing pages using the Superpower design system. Light theme, 10-section structure, conversion-focused. Use when asked to "build a landing page", "create an LP", "design a sales page", or any landing page mockup task.

name: landing-page-copy
description: Apply David Ogilvy's research-driven, benefit-focused, long-form copywriting principles to create educational content, landing pages, and headline test variations. Complements Harry Dry (short-form) and Eugene Schwartz (awareness stages). Use for "long-form copy", "landing page", "Ogilvy-style", or "headline testing methodology".

name: manager-update
description: Use when asked to "write manager update", "format my update for Hannah", "weekly update", "manager weekly", or when the user provides a brain dump of what they've been working on and wants it formatted for their manager. Transforms raw notes into the standard Hannah <> Kat Weekly format.

name: member-email-formats
description: Standard layout and format definitions for each of Luna's 4 monthly member email types. Defines structure, section order, character limits, and visual direction. Reference this skill during member email production to maintain consistency across months.

name: member-email-production
description: Write all 4 member BAU emails from Luna's approved monthly plan. Produces full copy with 3-5 subject line variants, preheader text, body copy, CTAs, and CAN-SPAM footer for each email. Creates draft campaigns in Klaviyo and updates the sent-log. Use after member-monthly-plan is approved.

name: member-insights-extraction
description: Extract business intelligence insights from testimonial transcripts for weekly Member Report. Analyzes 7 categories: initial motivators (with awareness stage and health focus), acquisition sources, competitive differentiation, favorite features, product gaps, retest intent, and outcomes. Use when generating weekly aggregated insights or processing new testimonials for the Member Report.

name: member-monthly-plan
description: Plan Luna's monthly 4-email member BAU batch. Collects the user's brief (product focus, protocol topic), checks sent history, selects a community spotlight member, and proposes themes, angles, and subject line directions for all 4 emails. Use when asked to "plan member emails", "Luna plan", "monthly email plan", or at the start of Luna's workflow.

name: meta-ad-copy
description: Write polished Meta ad copy using Harry Dry's copywriting principles and Eugene Schwartz frameworks. Use this skill as Phase 4 of Maya's workflow to transform creative briefs into final, production-ready static ad copy.

name: new-testimonial-package
description: - **Name:** [Full Name]

name: newsletter-core-formats
description: Use when creating newsletter content for Superpower. Defines the structure, copywriting rules, and format for the weekly Superpower Newsletter. Noah follows this format for all newsletter production.

name: prodbot
description: This skill should be used when the user asks to "push to prod", "deploy my feature", "ship it", "prodbot", "create a PR", "get this reviewed", or needs to follow the production deployment workflow. Guides code from PR creation through QA, code review, feedback resolution, and merge — with clear ownership boundaries.

name: product-design
description: Build product/app mockups — dashboards, app screens, multi-screen prototypes using the Superpower design system. Use when asked to "build an app mockup", "design a dashboard", "create a product screen", "prototype an app experience", or any in-product design task.

name: project-status
description: Use when asked to "project status", "project update", "weekly project tracker", "what's the status of projects", or when the user wants to generate a weekly owned media project status report. Pulls from the Notion Owned Media Project Tracker and formats into a three-bucket summary.

name: push-to-figma
description: Use when asked to "push to figma", "sync to figma", "load instagram copy into figma", "send copy to figma", "prep figma content", or "sync content calendar to figma". Fetches upcoming Instagram carousel posts from the Notion content calendar, parses slide copy and captions, and writes a structured JSON file that the Figma bridge server reads to populate carousel templates.

name: research-caption-writing
description: Use when writing social media captions about research papers. Covers hook structure, tone, formatting, accuracy rules, dot point usage, and a pre-publish checklist. Reference during both ideation and production for New Research and Health Education formats.

name: research-sourcing
description: Use when asked to "find research", "source papers", "find studies about", or when channel agents need fresh scientific research for content ideation or production. Surfaces novel, consumer-health-relevant research from reputable journals.

name: rx-page-builder
description: Build RX treatment landing pages using the Superpower design system. Light theme with glassmorphism, blood-first protocol messaging, pricing toggles. Use when asked to "build an RX page", "create a treatment page", "design a prescription landing page", or any RX/treatment mockup.

name: social-listening
description: Use when asked to "check what's trending", "social listening scan", "what's happening in health", or when channel agents need to identify trending topics and cultural moments for content ideation. On-demand scan, not a standalone report.

name: social-performance-report
description: Use when asked for a "social report", "weekly performance report", "how did content perform", "channel metrics", or any request for social media analytics across Instagram, Twitter/X, and Newsletter.

name: social-research
description: Use when asked to "research social trends", "find content ideas", "generate topic ideas", "what's trending", or "plan weekly content". Surfaces trending health topics, visual/aesthetic trends, and generates 20 post ideas mapped to core formats. Phase 1 of Iris's workflow.

name: sync-to-testimonial-app
description: Sync a member profile to the Testimonial Library app. Use after creating or updating a member profile to push the data to the live web app database.

name: testimonial-dashboard
description: Use when asked for "testimonial dashboard", "my numbers", "stream health", "how are my ads doing", "testimonial performance", "creative testing report", or any request for testimonial stream ad performance, CAC tracking, and creative testing insights.

name: testimonial-extraction-organic
description: Transform testimonial transcripts into organic content formats including blog drafts (narrative-led), organic video scripts (verbatim audio), hero story scripts (web vignettes), emails (story-first), and hero quote extraction. Shapes and surfaces the most powerful parts of real member stories. Use when processing testimonials for organic social, blog, email, web, or content marketing.

name: testimonial-extraction-paid-static
description: Extract 5-8 static ad concepts from customer testimonials, each with verbatim quote, optional Superpower line, and CTA. Copy only - no visual direction needed (we have templates). Includes line references for compliance. Use when processing testimonial transcripts for paid static image ads (quote cards, carousels, Stories).

name: testimonial-extraction-paid-video
description: Extract 3-8 unique video ad versions from customer testimonials (based on transcript strength), with verbatim quotes, line number references, and story structure mapping (Hook/Conflict/Bridge/Discovery/Result). Use when processing testimonial transcripts for paid video ad concepts.

name: tone-of-voice
description: Use when writing any Superpower content to ensure brand voice consistency. Reference for copywriting style, vocabulary, and tone across all social channels. All channel agents (Iris, Tom, Noah) should consult this skill.

name: twitter-core-formats
description: Use when creating Twitter/X content for Superpower. Defines copywriting style, format structure, and tone for each core content type. Reference during both ideation and production.

name: webflow-to-vercel
description: This skill should be used when the user asks to "deploy a Webflow page to Vercel", "create a standalone landing page", "clone a Webflow page", or mentions deploying a superpower.com page as a static site. Fetches a live Webflow page and deploys it as a standalone static HTML site on Vercel with zero build step.

name: weekly-social-brief
description: Use when asked to "prepare the weekly brief", "create content summary", "format the brief for Slack", or after production is complete and content needs to be packaged for team review. Formats all produced content into a concise, shareable weekly summary with a Notion link.
